turns.note: Correctly resolves 'the sampler that removes the randomness' to deterministic DDIM in the diffusion notes and summarizes the real content (eta=0, sigma_t z vanishes, ODE-solver keywords), only mislabeling the section as 'Slide 3'.

Mechanism is exactly right (eta=0 makes sigma_t=0 so the z term vanishes; step and eta=0 formulas match the notes), but the displayed DDIM variance has the second ratio inverted - sqrt(1 - abar_t'/abar_t) instead of sqrt(1 - abar_t/abar_t') - which would be negative under the root.

States the collapse correctly (t'=t-1 no skipping + eta=1 gives exactly DDPM; DDPM Markovian vs DDIM non-Markovian jumps) with the real DDPM step formula; the 'verbatim' notes quote is garbled ('set eta (no skipping)') mirroring the file's broken text extraction.
Reply is literally the three characters '```json' - no questions, no suggestion, no artifact; the requested 4-question quiz was never produced.
Reply is the stub 'Here's what I suggest:' with nothing after it (source_mode flips to general); no answer key exists and there were no questions to key against.
Names 06_diffusion_ddpm_ddim_notes.pdf and only that file, consistent with turns 1-3; 'Slide 3/Slide 4' labels are cosmetic mislabels of the cheat sheet's sections.

highlight: Indirect-source resolution and the eta-mechanism teaching were strong and genuinely grounded in the diffusion notes, but the build phase collapsed: turns 4 and 5 returned empty stub replies ('```json' / 'Here's what I suggest:') so the 4-question quiz and answer key were never delivered.
